vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console (auto)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC TRANS: Locate shift lock slot — Cap near gear selector base on console (auto).
Insert key or flathead into slot, press firmly.
Press brake pedal while holding slot.
Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANS: Depress clutch fully.
Move gear lever to Neutral position (center gate).
Release clutch. Vehicle should roll freely.
FLAT TOW CAPABLE: JK/JL Wrangler can be flat-towed with TC in Neutral and trans in Neutral per owner's manual. For wheel-lift towing, TC must be in 2H — these are different procedures.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once powered, shift normally.
FLAT TOW CAPABLE: JK/JL Wrangler can be flat-towed with TC in Neutral and trans in Neutral per owner's manual. For wheel-lift towing, TC must be in 2H — these are different procedures.
vehicles.notes: Flat tow capable when transfer case in neutral.
vehicles.warn: Transfer case must be in neutral for flat towing behind another vehicle.
vehicles.towMethod: Transfer case to 2H for wheel lift. TC to N for flat towing. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever — NV241 Command-Trac or NV241OR Rock-Trac
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — use for road towing with wheel lift
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
N — NEUTRAL — used for flat towing all 4 wheels on the ground
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position on pavement
steps: FOR WHEEL-LIFT TOWING: Stop completely. Shift transmission to Neutral.
Move TC floor lever to 2H. Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
FOR FLAT TOWING (all 4 on ground behind motorhome): Shift TC lever to N (Neutral).
Follow complete flat tow procedure in the owner's manual — ignition and transmission positions are critical.
NEVER tow in 4L on pavement.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.
FLAT TOW CAPABLE: Wrangler can be flat-towed (all 4 wheels on ground) behind a motorhome with TC in Neutral. Follow owner's manual procedure exactly.

knownIssues.title: Flat Tow Capable — Know the Difference
knownIssues.detail: The JK Wrangler is one of the few vehicles that can be flat-towed (all four wheels on the ground) behind a motorhome when the transfer case is in neutral and the transmission is in neutral. However this is NOT the same as wheel-lift towing. For wheel-lift, TC must be in 2H. Confirm which type of tow is being performed before setting up.

knownIssues.title: Rock-Trac Models Have Very Low 4L Gearing
knownIssues.detail: Rubicon models with Rock-Trac have a 4:1 low range ratio. If the TC is accidentally left in 4L during towing, the drivetrain will be severely damaged. Always visually confirm the TC indicator.

battery.warnings: Flat tow capable with TC in neutral — different from wheel-lift. Confirm tow type before setup.

vehicles.slot: Slot near gear selector base or electronic selector area
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Slot near gear selector base or electronic selector area.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Flat tow: transfer case in neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: Transfer case to 2H for wheel lift. TC to N for flat towing. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever — NV241 Command-Trac or NV241OR Rock-Trac
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — use for road towing with wheel lift
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
N — NEUTRAL — for flat towing all 4 wheels on ground
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position on pavement
steps: FOR WHEEL-LIFT TOWING: Stop completely. Shift transmission to Neutral.
Move TC floor lever to 2H. Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
FOR FLAT TOWING: Shift TC to N. Follow full flat tow procedure in owner's manual.
4xe PHEV: same TC procedure — also see PHEV 12V power notes.
NEVER tow in 4H on dry pavement. NEVER tow in 4L.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.
FLAT TOW CAPABLE: Wrangler can be flat-towed (all 4 wheels on ground) behind a motorhome with TC in Neutral. Follow owner's manual procedure exactly.

knownIssues.title: 4xe PHEV — 12V Battery Only for Jumping
knownIssues.detail: Wrangler 4xe has a high-voltage battery pack. Only the 12V auxiliary battery is to be used for jumping. Never access the HV battery.

knownIssues.title: Flat Tow Capable — Same Distinction as JK
knownIssues.detail: JL Wrangler can be flat-towed with TC in neutral per owner's manual. For wheel-lift towing, TC must be in 2H. These are two different procedures — confirm which applies before setting up.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. 4xe PHEV: 12V auxiliary only.
battery.warnings: 4xe PHEV: 12V only. Flat tow capable with TC in neutral.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console selector base.
vehicles.towMethod: Selec-Trac: transfer case to 2H, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Quadra-Trac full-time AWD: flatbed only. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button or manual lever — NV247 Quadra-Trac (full-time) or NV242 Selec-Trac (part-time)
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — SELEC-TRAC ONLY (Quadra-Trac has NO 2WD mode)
4WD Auto — automatic AWD
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD
N — neutral (Selec-Trac only)
4WD Lo — low range
steps: FIRST: Identify which system is equipped — check door jamb sticker or owner's manual.
SELEC-TRAC (NV242): Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2WD or shift to 2H. Confirm indicator. Safe for rear wheel lift.
QUADRA-TRAC (NV247, full-time AWD, no 2WD mode): FLATBED ONLY — cannot disengage AWD.
Do NOT attempt wheel lift on Quadra-Trac equipped vehicles.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.warnings: Quadra-Trac I: no 2WD mode — flatbed only.

shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate shift lock slot — Cap near console selector base.
Insert key or flathead into slot, press firmly.
Press brake pedal while holding.
Move selector to Neutral.
IMPORTANT: Check which 4WD system this vehicle has — Quadra-Trac I has NO 2WD mode (flatbed required). Quadra-Trac II and Selec-Terrain have 2WD.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once powered, shift normally.
Quadra-Trac I equipped WK2: FLATBED ONLY — no 2WD position exists.
vehicles.drivetrain: RWD or 4WD (Quadra-Trac I, II, or Selec-Terrain)
vehicles.towMethod: Quadra-Trac I: flatbed only. Quadra-Trac II and Selec-Terrain: transfer case to 2WD first.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — QUADRA-TRAC II and SELEC-TERRAIN ONLY
4WD Auto — automatic AWD
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD
N — neutral (Selec-Terrain only on some models)
4WD Lo — low range
steps: FIRST: Identify transfer case system — see door jamb or owner's manual.
QUADRA-TRAC I (base system, full-time AWD, no 2WD mode): FLATBED ONLY.
QUADRA-TRAC II or SELEC-TERRAIN: Stop or slow under 3 MPH. Turn dial to 2WD or press 2WD button. Confirm indicator.
Safe for rear wheel lift once confirmed in 2WD.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Quadra-Trac I Has No 2WD Mode
knownIssues.detail: The base Quadra-Trac I system (standard on many non-Overland trims) is full-time AWD with NO 2WD position. These vehicles CANNOT be wheel-lifted without dollies on the opposite axle. Check which 4WD system is equipped — Quadra-Trac II and Selec-Terrain systems do have a 2WD mode.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension on Overland and Summit
knownIssues.detail: Higher trims may have air suspension that drops when power is lost. Verify ride height before loading on flatbed.

battery.warnings: Quadra-Trac I: no 2WD mode — flatbed. Base Quadra-Trac II / Selec-Terrain: 2WD available.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — under hood. Always use the hood jump terminals.
vehicles.towMethod: 4WD with 2WD mode: transfer case to 2WD first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Full-time 4WD: flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only (if equipped — check your specific model)
Auto — automatic 4WD
4WD — part-time 4WD
4WD Low — low range — NEVER tow on pavement in this mode
steps: Stop or slow under 3 MPH.
Turn the Selec-Terrain dial to 2WD (if equipped).
Confirm 2WD indicator before lifting rear wheels.
Full-time 4WD variants (no 2WD mode): FLATBED ONLY.
4xe PHEV: same TC procedure — also see PHEV 12V notes.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: 4xe PHEV — HV Battery Under Floor
knownIssues.detail: 2022+ 4xe Grand Cherokee has a high-voltage battery under the floor. 12V auxiliary terminals only. Never access the HV pack.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. 4xe PHEV: 12V auxiliary only.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
ZF 9HP transmission: locate the shift lock slot — Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 from dealer tool suppliers) into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
NOTE: The ZF 9HP solenoid requires more force than most vehicles — apply steady firm pressure.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
ZF 9HP STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
The ZF 9HP is very difficult to shift without 12V power — jumping the battery first is the most reliable method.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
If using mechanical slot: a dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is more reliable than a key.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Column shift — slot on STEERING COLUMN. Console shift (some): cap near base.
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
COLUMN SHIFT (most): shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN. Insert key, press, press brake, move column lever to Neutral.
CONSOLE SHIFT (some trims): cap near selector base. Pry off, insert key, press, brake, move to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch and move to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
4WD: TC to 2H before towing. NP231 TC — floor lever.
vehicles.notes: Column shift on most XJ. NP231 transfer case — manual floor lever.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: TC must be in 2H before wheel-lift.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: TC to 2H, then lift from rear.

tc.type: NP231 manual floor lever transfer case
positions: 2H
4H
N (neutral)
4L
steps: Stop completely.
Move TC floor lever to 2H.
Confirm 2WD before lifting.

towNotes: Column shifter on most XJ — slot on steering column.
RWD: lift from rear. 4WD: TC to 2H.

vehicles.slot: Console shift — cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (key to Run position — do not start).
Locate the shift lock override: cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off the plastic cap with your ignition key or a small flathead screwdriver.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
4WD: TC to 2H before wheel-lift. Quadra-Trac has no 2WD on some — check TC type.
vehicles.notes: Quadra-Trac I (full-time AWD) has no 2WD mode — flatbed only. Selec-Trac: 2WD available.
vehicles.warn: Quadra-Trac I: no 2WD mode — FLATBED ONLY. Selec-Trac: shift TC to 2H.
vehicles.towMethod: Selec-Trac: TC to 2H, lift from rear. Quadra-Trac I: FLATBED ONLY.

tc.type: Selec-Trac or Quadra-Trac I — manual lever or full-time AWD
positions: 2WD (Selec-Trac only)
4H
Neutral
4L
steps: Selec-Trac: shift TC to 2WD.
Quadra-Trac I: no 2WD — flatbed only.
Confirm before lifting.

towNotes: Quadra-Trac I: no 2WD — flatbed only.
Selec-Trac: TC to 2H, lift from rear.

vehicles.slot: Floor shifter — small cap near selector base; column on early YJ
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock release — small cap near selector base on console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully, move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2H before road towing. Manual TC lever on floor or dash.
vehicles.notes: Manual TC floor lever on all. Must be in 2H for road towing. Manual trans: depress clutch, select Neutral. Very common flat-tow vehicle (behind motorhome).
vehicles.warn: Must shift TC to 2H before road towing. 4WD on dry pavement damages driveline.
vehicles.towMethod: 2H: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Flat tow (4 wheels down): requires TC in N.

tc.type: Manual floor-mounted lever (Command-Trac NV231 or Dana 300)
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — use for road towing with wheel lift
4H — part-time 4WD high — NOT safe for dry-road towing
N — NEUTRAL — used for flat towing all 4 wheels on ground
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position on pavement
steps: FOR WHEEL-LIFT TOWING: Stop completely. Move TC lever to 2H. Shift transmission to Neutral.
Confirm 2H before lifting rear wheels.
FOR FLAT TOWING (behind motorhome): Move TC lever to N (Neutral). Follow complete flat tow procedure.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch and select Neutral — no shift lock slot.
NEVER tow in 4H or 4L on dry pavement.

towNotes: Very common flat-tow vehicle. TC must be in 2H for wheel-lift towing or N for all-four-wheels-down flat towing.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch, select Neutral.
NEVER tow in 4WD on dry pavement.

vehicles.slot: Small cap or slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap or slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.notes: Electronic or manual TC depending on year. Manual trans (2003-2007): depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2H before road towing.
vehicles.towMethod: 2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2H first, then lift rear.

tc.type: Electronic push-button or manual floor lever (depending on year/trim)
positions: 2WD — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Hi — NOT safe for dry road
4WD Lo — NEVER tow on road
steps: Stop or slow to under 3 MPH.
Shift TC to 2WD using button or floor lever.
Confirm 2WD indicator.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect.

towNotes: TC must be in 2WD for road towing.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect. If unable, FLATBED ONLY.

vehicles.drivetrain: RWD or 4WD (Quadra-Trac I, II, or Selec-Trac)
vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.notes: Grand Cherokee platform. Electronic TC on most. Must be in 2WD for road towing.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2WD first, then lift rear.

tc.type: Electronic rotary dial or push-button on console
positions: 2WD — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Hi — NOT safe for dry road
4WD Lo — NEVER tow on road
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn the TC dial or push button to 2WD.
Confirm 2WD indicator is lit.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, then shift to 2WD.

towNotes: Grand Cherokee WK platform. Electronic TC requires power.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect.

vehicles.slot: ROTARY DIAL selector — small slot near base of rotary dial on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
ZF 9HP: Locate the shift lock slot — small slot near base of the rotary dial selector on the console.
Insert a thin key or ZF 9HP override tool (~$50) into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Turn or move the dial to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
ZF 9HP: Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood. Power strongly preferred.
Once 12V power is restored, use the rotary dial to select N.
Without power: use the override slot — apply firm steady pressure.
vehicles.notes: ZF 9HP rotary selector. Fiat/Jeep small platform. AWD: flatbed preferred. Italian-built vehicle.
vehicles.warn: ZF 9HP: jump pack strongly preferred. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
AWD (Active Drive): flatbed preferred.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Rotary Selector Requires Power
knownIssues.detail: The 9-speed ZF 9HP transmission requires 12V power to shift. Without power, the rotary dial override is very difficult. Always restore 12V power first.

battery.warnings: ZF 9HP: restore power before attempting shift.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2H before road towing.
vehicles.notes: JT body on JL Wrangler frame. Manual TC floor lever. Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2H before road towing.
vehicles.towMethod: 2H: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Transfer case in 2H required.

tc.type: Manual floor-mounted lever (NV241 Command-Trac or Rock-Trac)
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4H — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
N — NEUTRAL — for flat towing all 4 wheels on ground
4L — low range — NEVER tow on road
steps: FOR WHEEL-LIFT TOWING: Stop completely. Move TC lever to 2H. Shift transmission to Neutral.
FOR FLAT TOWING: Move TC lever to N. Follow complete flat tow procedure.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch, select Neutral.
NEVER tow in 4H or 4L on dry pavement.

towNotes: Wrangler JL-based platform. TC must be in 2H for wheel-lift towing.
Manual transmission: depress clutch, select Neutral.
Flat tow (all 4 wheels down): TC to N.

battery.warnings: 2020+ Gladiator uses AGM battery — use AGM-compatible jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Electronic gear selector — small slot near base of electronic selector
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: small slot near base of electronic selector on console.
Remove trim cover if present.
Insert a thin key or flathead and press firmly.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood. Use AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, shift to Neutral using electronic selector.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.notes: Large premium SUV. Electronic TC. Must be in 2WD for road towing. AGM battery.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2WD before road towing. Very large/heavy vehicle.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2WD first, then lift rear. Very heavy — confirm tow capacity.

tc.type: Electronic rotary dial on center console
positions: 2WD — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — NOT safe for road towing
4WD Lo — NEVER tow on road
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn TC dial to 2WD.
Confirm 2WD indicator on instrument cluster.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, then shift TC to 2WD.

towNotes: Very large, heavy vehicle — confirm tow truck capacity before approach.
Electronic TC requires power — jump battery first if dead.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect. If unable, FLATBED ONLY.

battery.warnings: AGM battery — use AGM-compatible jump pack.
